item.title: dam
item.content:encoded: thomas graham sure is a dick huh

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've read many SCPs. Many ones instilling cosmic fear, transhuman dread, divine malevolence. Yet…they didn't really scare me. I never truly thought "Oh no." I never was really spooked by it. This isn't an insult to their writing ability or me trying to brag about not getting scared- it's just it seemed to out there. I know there isn't an unstoppable god just waiting to break out of a door and destroy us, I know there isn't a stairwell with a disembodied head floating through it, I know there isn't a box from the future with unimaginable horror within.

But this? This was honestly, entirely, disturbingly close. I was honestly afraid when I read the last message, him describing the amnestics now being distributed through the ventilation, that was scary.

This was fucked up. +1

item.title: Re: Technical Captain Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Noted, my apologies for the inconvenience. It won't happen again. My SCP-4260 most likely suffered a similar problem, so I've removed its tags as well.

item.title: Technical Captain Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Author, I removed all of your extraneous non-applicable tags.

Please do not add tags yourself if you're not sure of what you're doing. Wait for one of the technical team to handle it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm happy you enjoyed! I made the correction you suggested, and thanks a lot for that!

If you want to read more, my SCP-4051 is about humanoid abuse as well, albeit a bit longer. I think that, including the tale that completes his story, it's about 12k words?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Woah, this one threw me for a quick loop. Fucking Ethics committee. The only comment I have is near the end of Natalie's letter I'm pretty sure it should be "have begun to take notice…"

Otherwise, this is a pretty unsettling piece, really highlighting how shitty some versions of the Foundation treat their anomalous persons

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Are you referring to the blinking collapsible, or the blinking text inside the collapsible? I grabbed both from other articles, but I'll see what I can do.

Edit: Just occurred to me you may be referring to the false collapsibles in the addendum. Those are intentional, given the reader only possesses 2/4415 clearance and can't view them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The java script is bugging on me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If you're interested in the characters appearing in this article, check out below:

  • SCP-4051: SCP-4051, The Beast Beneath The Library, SCP-4260
  • Samuel Veras: He Who Screws With Reality
  • Thomas Graham: SCP-4051, The Beast Beneath The Library, SCP-4175
  • Natalie Reems: APAS

Thanks for reading!
